Petition For Approval Of Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement For Costs Advanced
Off Calendar Jun-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore, via ZOOM, for LaMarcus Gregory, Conservatee; James Bridgman, via ZOOM, for Janice Rodgers, Trustee.

The court begins by asking Mr. Bridgman if he still objects to this petition. Mr. Bridgman confirms that he does especially since this is a special needs trust and that most of the funds in the trust should be used for Mr. Gregory's care and not to charge for time filing a preemptory challenge, for example. Mr. Moore reports that he and his firm have Mr. Gregory's best interest in mind. Mr. Moore provides additional oral testimony to support his petition. Mr. Bridgman provides additional oral testimony to support his objection to the petition.

The court summarizes counsel's positions and the Petition for Approval of Payment of Attorney Fees, etc., is taken under submission by the court.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Payment Of Attorneys? Fees And Reimbursement For Costs Advanced

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore appeared by zoom for Lamarcus Gregory (Beneficiary).
James Bridgman appeared by zoom for Janice Rogers (Trustee).

The Court asked Mr. Bridgman if he has any support for the second receipt in the amount of $1,074.66 from the estate of Tony Gregory. Mr. Bridgman stated that he believes it relates to Tony's estate, that he has no control over that estate, that all he knows it that money was deposited and asked that any irregularity be waived. The Court asked Mr. Bridgman if he has anything he'd like to add regarding the additional time spent to amend the petition when the petition was so completely inadequate. Mr. Bridgman stated that they spent an extra three hours on the amendment and that he leaves it to the Court's discretion. The Court informed Mr. Bridgman that it received portions of the bank statement and that it needs to be complete. Mr. Bridgman stated that he will try to drop a copy of the bank statement to the Court tomorrow. The Court questions why nothing has been spent on Lamarcus' needs. Mr. Bridgman stated that Lamarcus is in a good situation, that he shares an apartment with his Girlfriend, that his social security disability benefit covers all of his expenses and that Ms. Rogers checks in with Lamarcus every month or so. Mr. Moore stated that he is not objecting, that he has not been able to reach his Client, that Lamarcus needs dental work and raised the issue of the accounting showing that his Client made that request, but it was denied. Mr. Moore also raised the issue of having a plan in place going forward to address any expenses or requests, stated that his fee request is still pending and asked the Court to pass on the issue of fees until his fee petition is heard. Mr. Bridgman stated that they have been trying to get Lamarcus to provide contact information for someone who will do the dental work and that they suggested going to the dental school. Mr. Moore stated that he can try and assist, but he is having a hard time with contact and asked that Ms. Rogers provide assistance. The Amended First Account and Report of Trustee is under submission.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Appearances: Christopher Moore appeared by zoom for Lamarcus Gregory (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it received a peremptory challenge from Mr. Moore, that it accepts the challenge and that it will continue the matter to one of Judge Moody's calendars. Mr. Bridgman stated that he has nothing to address with respect to the challenge and requested a further out continuance stating that they have a list of things to work on. The First Account and Report of Trustee is continued to January 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Bond And The Fully Executed Lamarcus Gregory Special Needs Trust
Off Calendar Aug-05-2019 Continued To Aug-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore appeared by telephone for LaMarcus Gregory.

Mr. Moore stated that he has been in communication with the Trustee (Janice) and her Attorney James Bridgman, that he was informed last week that they need a little more time to get the bond and execute the special needs trust, and that he does not have specifics as to why it has taken this amount of time. Status on filing of the bond and the executed special needs trust is continued to August 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The documents are to be filed by August 19, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Establishing And Directing Funding Of A Special Needs Trust For The Benefit Of Lamarcus Gregory And For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore for LaMarcus Gregory.
Kenneth Gregory (Brother) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Gregory stated that Attorney James Bridgman took the case in 2017, that he did not see a substitution of attorney, that he does not know if Mr. Bridgman got paid, that LaMarcus agreed to an amount and that it went from $6,000.00 to $13,000.00. Mr. Moore stated that it is his understanding that Mr. Bridgman represented Janice Rogers as the Administrator of Ernestine Harris' estate, that Mr. Bridgman tried to have the trust established in that estate, and that Mr. Bridgman was told by the Court that Mr. Gregory had to do it on his own and have separate Counsel. Mr. Moore also stated that the only thing that has changed was the removal of the Trust Advisory Committee, that Sandy Knox is a friend of LaMarcus and he asked for her to be a beneficiary, that LaMarcus had a relationship with Sandy and he wants to provide for her, that there are remainder beneficiaries and that the fees are at the Court's discretion. The Amended Petition for Order Establishing a Special Needs Trust for the Benefit of LaMarcus Gregory, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Establishing And Directing Funding Of A Special Needs Trust For The Benefit Of Lamarcus Gregory And For Attorneys' Fees
Off Calendar May-13-2019 Continued To Jun-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Moore for LaMarcus Gregory.

Mr. Moore stated that the proposed trustee does consent to serve and addressed the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes. Discussion was held with regard to whether or not a Trust Advisory Committee and Trust Protector are required, removing language from the trust with respect to the Court ordering/directing payment from the two representatives of the related estates into the trust, removing language with respect to the trust terminating
21-years after death of beneficiary, the appropriate amount of bond, whether or not the petition needs to be amended, etc. The Petition for Order Establishing and Directing Funding of a Special Needs Trust, etc. is continued to June 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a declaration in response to the Probate Examiner's notes and for Counsel to make a determination as to whether he needs to amend his petition.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
